page: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores operating at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store, and it supports a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and specialized processing capabilities. The instance achieved a strong rating in Geekbench multi-core performance with a score of 15999.0, and a Passmark CPU score of 48532.6. It also demonstrates strong performance in database operations with a score of 21256.09 and static web serving throughput at 41.18 GB/sec. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES flags, yielding a strong Passmark CPU encryption score of 43553.35.

While the instance excels in small-scale LLM inference, processing 2270.5 tokens per second on a 135M model, it exhibits a performance tradeoff on larger models, dropping to a weak rating of 2.72 tokens per second for text generation on a 70B model due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Given the high-speed local NVMe storage and balanced compute-to-memory ratio, this instance is highly suited for database hosting, static web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring fast local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 15999.0 and strong database operations. While it performs exceptionally well in static web serving and small-scale LLM inference, it shows a clear performance tradeoff on larger LLM workloads due to the absence of GPU hardware. This instance is optimized for database workloads, web serving, and applications requiring high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 2850 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge features 48 Intel vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 2850 GB NVMe SSD for high-performance database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-speed local NVMe storage, dedicated Intel Xeon processors, and strong multi-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz
192.0 GB of DDR4 system memory paired with a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Strong benchmark performance in multi-core processing, database operations, and static web serving
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Well-suited for database hosting, web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise applications
